practice.name: Problem 7 (1985 AJHSME)
practice.statement: A ``stair-step'' figure is made of alternating black and white squares in each row. Rows $1$ through $4$ are shown. All rows begin and end with a white square. The number of black squares in the $37$th row is

practice.name: Problem 8 (2017 AMC 10A)
practice.statement: At a gathering of $30$ people, there are $20$ people who all know each other and $10$ people who know no one. People who know each other hug, and people who do not know each other shake hands. How many handshakes occur?

practice.name: Problem 19 (2017 AMC 10A)
practice.statement: Alice refuses to sit next to either Bob or Carla. Derek refuses to sit next to Eric. How many ways are there for the five of them to sit in a row of $5$ chairs under these conditions?

practice.name: Problem 15 (1985 AJHSME)
practice.statement: How many whole numbers between $100$ and $400$ contain the digit $2$?

practice.name: Problem 9 (1986 AJHSME)
practice.statement: Using only the paths and the directions shown, how many different routes are there from $M$ to $N$? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 4 (1988 AJHSME)
practice.statement: The figure consists of alternating light and dark squares. The number of dark squares exceeds the number of light squares by [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 16 (1988 AJHSME)
practice.statement: Placing no more than one $X$ in each small square, what is the greatest number of $X$'s that can be put on the grid shown without getting three $X$'s in a row vertically, horizontally, or diagonally? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 25 (1988 AJHSME)
practice.statement: A palindrome is a whole number that reads the same forwards and backwards. If one neglects the colon, certain times displayed on a digital watch are palindromes. Three examples are: $1:01, 4:44$, and $12:21$. How many times during a $12$-hour period will be palindromes?

practice.name: Problem 12 (1990 AJHSME)
practice.statement: There are twenty-four $4$-digit numbers that use each of the four digits $2, 5, 7$, and $4$ exactly once. Listed in numerical order from smallest to largest, the number in the $17^{\text{th}}$ position in the list is

practice.name: Problem 25 (1990 AJHSME)
practice.statement:
How many different patterns can be made by shading exactly two of the nine squares? Patterns that can be matched by flips and/or turns are not considered different. For example, the patterns shown below are not considered different.

[See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 5 (1991 AJHSME)
practice.statement: A "domino" is made up of two small squares: [See figure in original PDF]. Which of the "checkerboards" illustrated below CANNOT be covered exactly and completely by a whole number of non-overlapping dominoes?

practice.name: Problem 11 (1991 AJHSME)
practice.statement: There are several sets of three different numbers whose sum is $15$ which can be chosen from $\{1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9\}$. How many of these sets contain a $5$?

practice.name: Problem 7 (1992 AJHSME)
practice.statement: The digit-sum of $998$ is $9+9+8=26$. How many $3$-digit whole numbers, whose digit-sum is $26$, are even?

practice.name: Problem 22 (1993 AJHSME)
practice.statement: Pat Peano has plenty of $0$'s, $1$'s, $3$'s, $4$'s, $5$'s, $6$'s, $7$'s, $8$'s and $9$'s, but he has only twenty-two $2$'s. How far can he number the pages of his scrapbook with these digits?

practice.name: Problem 8 (1994 AJHSME)
practice.statement: For how many three-digit whole numbers does the sum of the digits equal $25$?

practice.name: Problem 24 (1994 AJHSME)
practice.statement: A $2$ by $2$ square is divided into four $1$ by $1$ squares. Each of the small squares is to be painted either green or red. In how many different ways can the painting be accomplished so that no green square shares its top or right side with any red square? There may be as few as zero or as many as four small green squares.

practice.name: Problem 23 (1995 AJHSME)
practice.statement: How many four-digit whole numbers are there such that the leftmost digit is odd, the second digit is even, and all four digits are different?

practice.name: Problem 21 (1996 AJHSME)
practice.statement: How many subsets containing three different numbers can be selected from the set $\{89, 95, 99, 132, 166, 173\}$ so that the sum of the three numbers is even?

practice.name: Problem 17 (1997 AJHSME)
practice.statement: A cube has eight vertices (corners) and twelve edges. A segment, such as $x$, which joins two vertices not joined by an edge is called a diagonal. Segment $y$ is also a diagonal. How many diagonals does a cube have? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 4 (1998 AJHSME)
practice.statement: How many triangles are in this figure? (Some triangles may overlap other triangles.) [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 15 (1999 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Bicycle license plates in Flatville each contain three letters. The first is chosen from the set $\{C, H, L, P, R\}$, the second from $\{A, I, O\}$, and the third from $\{D, M, N, T\}$. When Flatville needed more license plates, they added two new letters. The new letters may both be added to one set or one letter may be added to one set and one to another set. What is the largest possible number of ADDITIONAL license plates that can be made by adding two letters?

practice.name: Problem 14 (2001 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Tyler has entered a buffet line in which he chooses one kind of meat, two different vegetables and one dessert. If the order of food items is not important, how many different meals might he choose? Meat: beef, chicken, pork. Vegetables: baked beans, corn, potatoes, tomatoes. Dessert: brownies, chocolate cake, chocolate pudding, ice cream.

practice.name: Problem 24 (2001 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Each half of this figure is composed of $3$ red triangles, $5$ blue triangles and $8$ white triangles. When the upper half is folded down over the centerline, $2$ pairs of red triangles coincide, as do $3$ pairs of blue triangles. There are $2$ red-white pairs. How many white pairs coincide? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 19 (2002 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many whole numbers between $99$ and $999$ contain exactly one $0$?

practice.name: Problem 16 (2003 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Ali, Bonnie, Carlo, and Dianna are going to drive together to a nearby theme park. The car they are using has $4$ seats: $1$ Driver seat, $1$ front passenger seat, and $2$ back passenger seats. Bonnie and Carlo are the only ones who know how to drive the car. How many possible seating arrangements are there?

practice.name: Problem 18 (2003 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Each of the twenty dots on the graph below represents one of Sarah's classmates. Classmates who are friends are connected with a line segment. For her birthday party, Sarah is inviting only the following: all of her friends and all of those classmates who are friends with at least one of her friends. How many classmates will not be invited to Sarah's party? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 2 (2004 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many different four-digit numbers can be formed by rearranging the four digits in $2004$?

practice.name: Problem 4 (2004 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Lance, Sally, Joy and Fred are chosen for the team. In how many ways can the three starters be chosen?

practice.name: Problem 8 (2004 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Find the number of two-digit positive integers whose digits total $7$.

practice.name: Problem 14 (2005 AMC 8)
practice.statement: The Little Twelve Basketball Conference has two divisions, with six teams in each division. Each team plays each of the other teams in its own division twice and every team in the other division once. How many conference games are scheduled?

practice.name: Problem 18 (2005 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many three-digit numbers are divisible by $13$?

practice.name: Problem 21 (2005 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many distinct triangles can be drawn using three of the dots below as vertices? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 11 (2006 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many two-digit numbers have digits whose sum is a perfect square?

practice.name: Problem 4 (2007 AMC 8)
practice.statement: A haunted house has six windows. In how many ways can Georgie the Ghost enter the house by one window and leave by a different window?

practice.name: Problem 14 (2008 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Three A's, three B's, and three C's are placed in the nine spaces of a $3 \times 3$ grid so that each row and column contain one of each letter. If A is placed in the upper left corner, how many arrangements are possible? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 16 (2009 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many $3$-digit positive integers have digits whose product equals $24$?

practice.name: Problem 20 (2009 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many non-congruent triangles have vertices at three of the eight points in the array shown below? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 22 (2009 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many whole numbers between $1$ and $1000$ do not contain the digit $1$?

practice.name: Problem 25 (2010 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Everyday at school, Jo climbs a flight of $6$ stairs. Joe can take the stairs $1$, $2$, or $3$ at a time. For example, Jo could climb $3$, then $1$, then $2$. In how many ways can Jo climb the stairs?

practice.name: Problem 8 (2011 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Bag A has three chips labeled $1$, $3$, and $5$. Bag B has three chips labeled $2$, $4$, and $6$. If one chip is drawn from each bag, how many different values are possible for the sum of the two numbers on the chips?

practice.name: Problem 19 (2011 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many rectangles are in this figure? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 23 (2011 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many $4$-digit positive integers have four different digits, where the leading digit is not zero, the integer is a multiple of $5$, and $5$ is the largest digit?

practice.name: Problem 10 (2012 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many $4$-digit numbers greater than $1000$ are there that use the four digits of $2012$?

practice.name: Problem 14 (2012 AMC 8)
practice.statement: In the BIG N, a middle school football conference, each team plays every other team exactly once. If a total of $21$ conference games were played during the $2012$ season, how many teams were members of the BIG N conference?

practice.name: Problem 21 (2013 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Samantha lives $2$ blocks west and $1$ block south of the southwest corner of City Park. Her school is $2$ blocks east and $2$ blocks north of the northeast corner of City Park. On school days she bikes on streets to the southwest corner of City Park, then takes a diagonal path through the park to the northeast corner, and then bikes on streets to school. If her route is as short as possible, how many different routes can she take?

practice.name: Problem 22 (2013 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Toothpicks are used to make a grid that is $60$ toothpicks long and $32$ toothpicks wide. How many toothpicks are used altogether?

practice.name: Problem 11 (2014 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Jack wants to bike from his house to Jill's house, which is located three blocks east and two blocks north of Jack's house. After biking each block, Jack can continue either east or north, but he needs to avoid a dangerous intersection one block east and one block north of his house. In how many ways can he reach Jill's house by biking a total of five blocks?

practice.name: Problem 16 (2014 AMC 8)
practice.statement: The "Middle School Eight" basketball conference has $8$ teams. Every season, each team plays every other conference team twice (home and away), and each team also plays $4$ games against non-conference opponents. What is the total number of games in a season involving the "Middle School Eight" teams?

practice.name: Problem 4 (2015 AMC 8)
practice.statement: The Centerville Middle School chess team consists of $2$ boys and $3$ girls. A photographer wants to take a picture of the team to appear in the local newspaper. She decides to have them sit in a row with a boy at each end and the $3$ girls in the middle. How many such arrangements are possible?

practice.name: Problem 10 (2015 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many integers between $1000$ and $9999$ have $4$ distinct digits?

practice.name: Problem 12 (2015 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many pairs of parallel edges, such as $\overline{AB}$ and $\overline{GH}$ or $\overline{EH}$ and $\overline{FG}$, does a cube have? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 17 (2016 AMC 8)
practice.statement: An ATM password at Fred's Bank is composed of four digits from $0$ to $9$, with repeated digits allowable. If no password may begin with the sequence $9, 1, 1$, then how many passwords are possible?

practice.name: Problem 15 (2017 AMC 8)
practice.statement: In the arrangement of letters and numerals below, by how many different paths can one spell $\text{AMC}8$? Beginning at the $\text{A}$ in the middle, a path allows only moves from one letter to an adjacent (above, below, left, or right, but not diagonal) letter. One example of such a path is traced in the picture. [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 16 (2018 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Professor Chang has nine different language books lined up on a bookshelf: two Arabic, three German, and four Spanish. How many ways are there to arrange the nine books on the shelf keeping the Arabic books together and keeping the Spanish books together?

practice.name: Problem 19 (2018 AMC 8)
practice.statement: In a sign pyramid a cell gets a "$+" if the two cells below it have the same sign, and it gets a "$-$" if the two cells below it have different signs. The diagram below illustrates a sign pyramid with four levels. How many possible ways are there to fill the four cells in the bottom row to produce a "$+" at the top of the pyramid? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 7 (2020 AMC 8)
practice.statement: How many integers between $2020$ and $2400$ have four distinct digits arranged in increasing order? (For example, $2357$ is one such integer.)

practice.name: Problem 10 (2020 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Zara has a collection of $4$ marbles: an Aggie, a Bumblebee, a Steelie, and a Tiger. She wants to display them in a row on a shelf, but does not want to put the Steelie and the Tiger next to one another. In how many ways can she do this?

practice.name: Problem 21 (2020 AMC 8)
practice.statement: A game board consists of $64$ squares that alternate in color between black and white. The figure below shows square $P$ in the bottom row and square $Q$ in the top row. A marker is placed at $P$. A step consists of moving the marker onto one of the adjoining white squares in the row above. How many $7$-step paths are there from $P$ to $Q$? (The figure shows a sample path.) [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 14 (2022 AMC 8)
practice.statement: In how many ways can the letters in BEEKEEPER be rearranged so that two or more E's do not appear together?

practice.name: Problem 23 (2022 AMC 8)
practice.statement: A $\triangle$ or $\bigcirc$ is placed in each of the nine squares in a $3$-by-$3$ grid. Shown below is a sample configuration with three $\triangle$'s in a line. How many configurations will have three $\triangle$'s in a line and three $\bigcirc$'s in a line? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 16 (2023 AMC 8)
practice.statement: The letters P, Q, and R are entered in a $20 \times 20$ grid according to the pattern shown below. How many Ps, Qs, and Rs will appear in the completed table? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 21 (2023 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Alina writes the numbers $1, 2, \dots, 9$ on separate cards, one number per card. She wishes to divide the cards into $3$ groups of $3$ cards so that the sum of the numbers in each group will be the same. In how many ways can this be done?

practice.name: Problem 8 (2024 AMC 8)
practice.statement: On Monday Taye has $\$2$. Everyday he either gains $\$3$ or doubles the amount of money he had on the previous day. How many different dollar amounts could Taye have on Thursday, $3$ days later?

practice.name: Problem 13 (2024 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Buzz Bunny is hopping up and down a set of stairs, one step at a time. In how many ways can Buzz start on the ground, make a sequence of $6$ hops, and end up back on the ground? (For example, one sequence of hops is up-up-down-down-up-down.)

practice.name: Problem 17 (2024 AMC 8)
practice.statement: A chess king is said to "attack" all squares one step away from it (basically any square right next to it in any direction), horizontally, vertically, or diagonally. For instance, a king on the center square of a $3 \times 3$ grid attacks all $8$ other squares, as shown below. Suppose a white king and a black king are placed on different squares of $3 \times 3$ grid so that they do not attack each other. In how many ways can this be done? [See figure in original PDF]

practice.name: Problem 16 (2025 AMC 8)
practice.statement: Kei draws a $6 \times 6$ grid. He colors $13$ of the unit squares silver and the remaining squares gold. Kei then folds the grid in half vertically, forming pairs of overlapping unit squares. Let $m$ and $M$ be the least and greatest possible number of gold-on-gold pairs, respectively. What is $m+M$?

practice.name: Problem 22 (2025 AMC 8)
practice.statement: The Konigsberg School has assigned grades $1$ through $7$ to pods $A$ through $G$, one grade per pod. The school noticed that each pair of connected pods has been assigned grades differing by $2$ or more grade levels. (For example, grades $1$ and $2$ will not be in pods directly connected by a walkway.) What is the sum of the grade levels assigned to pods $C$, $E$, and $F$? [See figure in original PDF]
